[[Darrell Trindall]] (brother) }} }} {{BLP more citations needed|date=March 2022}} '''Steve Trindall''' born 23 April 1973 in [[Wee Waa, New South Wales]], Australia is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. Trindall's position of choice is as a {{rlp|pr}}. He played for the [[Canberra Raiders]], the [[North Sydney Bears]], the [[Northern Eagles]] and the [[Wests Tigers]] in Australia. In the [[Super League]] he played for the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] and also played for [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]]. ==Playing career== Trindall made his first grade debut for Canberra in Round 20 1993 against Cronulla. In 1996, Trindall joined North Sydney and became a regular starter in the team. Trindall played in North Sydney's preliminary final loss against St George. In 1997, Trindall made 25 appearances as Norths reached the preliminary final against Newcastle. A try by Norths player [[Michael Buettner]] levelled the scores at 12-12 and halfback [[Jason Taylor (rugby league)|Jason Taylor]] had the chance to kick a winning goal with 4 minutes left to play. A normally reliable kicker, Taylor missed the goal that would have sent Norths into their first grade final since 1943. In the following minutes, Newcastle kicked a field goal and scored a late try to win the match 17–12. This was Norths 4th preliminary final defeat in 7 years. In 1998, Norths finished 5th but were eliminated from the finals series after losing to Parramatta and Canterbury. In 1999, Trindall was a member of The North Sydney side which played its final ever first grade game against The [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in [[Townsville]].{{cite web | url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-1999/round-26/north-queensland-vs-north-sydney/summary.html | title=NRL 1999 - Round 26 - Rugby League Project }} After Norths merged with arch rivals Manly to form the [[Northern Eagles]], Trindall was one of the Norths players signed to join the new team. Trindall made 44 appearances for the club before departing the team to join [[Wests Tigers]]. Trindall then went on to play with Wests for 2 seasons before leaving the club in 2003 to join the [[London Broncos]]. Trindall played 64 times for London between 2003 and 2006 before finishing his career with Whitehaven. ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.nrlstats.com/archive/players.cfm?PlayerID=1111 NRL stats] *[http://stats.rleague.com/rl/scorers/players/Steve_Trindall NRL points]{{Dead link|date=September 2019 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }} *[http://www.rugbyleagueproject.com/players/Steve_Trindall.html Rugby League Project stats] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20080131084239/http://www.rleague.com/db/player/t/trindall_steve/index.php RLeague.com profile] {{DEFAULTSORT:Trindall, Steve}} [[Category:1973 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Australian rugby league players]] [[Category:Canberra Raiders players]] [[Category:London Broncos players]] [[Category:North Sydney Bears players]] [[Category:20th-century Australian sportsmen]] [[Category:Northern Eagles players]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Wee Waa]] [[Category:Rugby league props]] [[Category:Wests Tigers players]] [[Category:Whitehaven R.L.F.C. players]]
